mnemonics: Ljung-Box is a Ljung-Line for Autocorrelation: It checks if the 'line' of residuals is just random noise.
keyPoints: Co-developed the Ljung-Box Q test, a portmanteau test for autocorrelation in a time series.
The test is used to check if residuals from a model behave like white noise.
It is a refinement of the Box-Pierce test, using a chi-squared distribution for better small-sample properties.
commonMistakes: Using the test on the raw data instead of on the model's residuals.
Misinterpreting a high p-value as 'good'; a high p-value means no significant autocorrelation is found (which is the desired result for residuals).
Forgetting that the test's null hypothesis is that the first 'k' autocorrelations are jointly zero.
